title: Ticket 06 - UI tests & UX
description: Dummy data for ticket 06 - ui tests & ux (V01) in the DoomScrollQuiz.

questions.prompt: Ticket 06 centers on this working idea: "Plan and implement uI tests and UX clearly for a user interface in day-to-day project work." Which statement matches it best?

options.text: We should handle uI tests and UX in user-interface work clearly, document decisions, and implement them in a user-friendly way.
options.explanation: This summarizes the core idea behind uI tests and UX in user-interface work in a direct, practical way.

options.text: We can ignore uI tests and UX in user-interface work as long as the mockup looks acceptable.
options.explanation: Ui tests and ux in user-interface work should not be postponed or ignored.

options.text: Only designers are allowed to deal with uI tests and UX in user-interface work.
options.explanation: This makes the topic unnecessarily exclusive and misses the learning goal.

options.text: Ui tests and ux in user-interface work only matters after the implementation is already finished.
options.explanation: The learning idea should shape decisions during the ongoing project work.

questions.prompt: Which reaction best fits ticket 06 when working with uI tests and UX in user-interface work?

options.text: Clarify the goal briefly, document the decision, and make the next UI step explicit.
options.explanation: This combines alignment, transparency, and actionable implementation.

options.text: Skip documentation first and rely on memory once the sprint gets busy.
options.explanation: For uI tests and UX in user-interface work this would be imprecise and risky.

options.text: Raise issues only after they have already caused visible user frustration.
options.explanation: The better direction is to clarify early instead of reacting too late.

options.text: Avoid questions even if the user journey is still unclear.
options.explanation: The learning idea requires clarity, not silent guessing.
